sources.population: Postpartum women and newborns during the first six weeks following childbirth.
sources.clinical_setting: DoH-licensed facility, outpatient, home-visit, and teleconsultation postnatal services in Abu Dhabi.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for Abu Dhabi postnatal follow-up during the first six weeks; partial for later postpartum care and settings outside Abu Dhabi.

exact_sections.heading: 1. Standard Scope and postnatal check schedule
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines Abu Dhabi postnatal services and four checks through six weeks, with timing and mode adjusted for risk.

exact_sections.heading: 3.1 Core Components — maternal physical and emotional health, education, support, and continuity
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Requires maternal physical and emotional assessment, postpartum haemorrhage and hypertensive-risk screening, feeding support, family planning discussion, continuity, and EMR documentation.

exact_sections.heading: Appendix 1 — medical risk indicators for mothers
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists thromboembolic symptoms, heavy bleeding, wound infection signs, fever, abdominal pain, wound discharge, severe headache, visual symptoms, and postpartum hypertensive complications.

exact_sections.heading: Appendices 2 and 3 — detailed maternal postnatal and home-visit checks
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies vital signs, uterine involution, lochia, wound healing, bladder and bowel function, feeding, mood, support, family planning, pelvic floor, indicated tests, and documented follow-up.

archive.title: Postnatal Care Program Standards
archive.population: Postpartum women and newborns during the first six weeks following childbirth.
archive.setting: DoH-licensed facility, outpatient, home-visit, and teleconsultation postnatal services in Abu Dhabi.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: 1. Standard Scope and postnatal check schedule
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Defines Abu Dhabi postnatal services and four checks through six weeks, with timing and mode adjusted for risk.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: 3.1 Core Components — maternal physical and emotional health, education, support, and continuity

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Depression in adults: treatment and management — Recommendations
sources.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE mental-health, crisis, referral, and legal adaptation
sources.population: Adults with possible or diagnosed depression; communication and carer involvement are qualified.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary and mental healthcare recognition and assessment.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for documenting mood and interest screening, duration, course, function, social and interpersonal context, sleep, substances, medicines, loneliness and clinician-completed risk assessment. It does not diagnose depression or generate a referral or treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.7 — recognition, comprehensive assessment, function, and context
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting low mood, loss of interest, duration, course, functional, interpersonal and social difficulty, previous mood history, physical and mental comorbidity, sleep, stressful events, medicines, substances, loneliness and support.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.8–1.2.12 — clinician-completed risk assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording that suicidal ideation, intent, support and immediate risk were directly assessed by a competent clinician; it does not populate a negative, assign risk, or generate escalation.
